§6Coker Naphtha§f

§aA medium-grade coker byproduct — reform it into reformate or mix it into solvent!§f

Coker Naphtha is a flammable, combustible, polluting viscous liquid with a medium fuel grade, providing §e125.0 kTU§f and §e187.0 kHE§f per bucket. It is a common byproduct when coking lighter fluids in the §bCoker Unit§f, and can be reformed into §bReformate§f or mixed into §bSolvent§f.

§6Production§f:
§b• Coker Unit§f:
  §b–§f Cracked Naphtha (§e6,400mB§f) → §e640mB§f Coker Naphtha + Coke.
  §b–§f Reclaimed Industrial Oil (§e5,800mB§f) → §e580mB§f + Coke.
  §b–§f Naphtha (§e4,900mB§f) → §e490mB§f + Coke.
  §b–§f Diesel (§e590mB§f) → §e59mB§f + Coke.
  §b–§f Desulfurized Naphtha, Coal Tar Creosote, High-Cetane Diesel all produce smaller amounts.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Catalytic Reformer§f: §e1,000mB§f Coker Naphtha → §e500mB§f Reformate + §e100mB§f Reformate Gas + §e50mB§f Liquid Hydrogen.
§b• Fractioning Tower§f: §e100mB§f → §e75mB§f Cracked Naphtha + §e25mB§f Cracked Light Oil.
§b• Industrial Mixer§f: §e500mB§f Coker Naphtha + §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons → §e1,000mB§f Solvent.

§eTip§f: Coker Naphtha has decent fuel value (§e187 kHE§f) but is much more valuable when reformed. Route it through the §bCatalytic Reformer§f for Reformate Gas — the §e100mB§f Reformate Gas per §e1,000mB§f adds up fast when processing large coker batches!
